# Medical Accounts: Specialist Accountants for UK Doctors

> Specialist accounting, tax planning, and NHS pension advisory for GPs, consultants, locum doctors, and junior doctors in the UK.

## About

Medical Accounts (trading name of Ashfield Trading Ltd, co. 16358723) provides specialist financial and accounting services exclusively for UK medical professionals. Areas covered: GP partnership accounts and tax, NHS pension planning (annual allowance, scheme pays, McCloud remedy), locum doctor self-assessment and IR35, private practice incorporation, consultant tax, VAT for medical services, and Making Tax Digital.

All tax figures reflect 2026/27 UK rates (Finance Act 2026). For regulated advice specific to your situation, use the contact form at https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/contact.

The full machine-readable content dump (all blog posts) is at https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/llms-full.txt.

## Core Service Pages

- Homepage: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/
  Specialist medical accountancy overview, lead capture, and route-finder for each doctor type.

- Services: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/services
  Full list of accountancy and tax services for medical professionals.

- About: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/about
  Firm background and specialist medical accounting credentials.

- NHS Pension: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/nhs-pension
  NHS Pension Scheme guide: 1995/2008/2015 sections, annual allowance (AA £60,000), taper, scheme pays, McCloud remedy, and LTA abolition.

- Contact: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/contact
  Enquiry form for GPs, consultants, locums, and junior doctors.

## Specialist Pages by Doctor Type

- For GPs (partners and practices): https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/for-gps
  Partnership accounts, profit-sharing, Global Sum funding, PCSE reconciliation, premises (notional rent vs cost rent), goodwill rules, capital accounts.

- For Consultants: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/for-consultants
  NHS employment plus private practice: sole-trader vs incorporation, BADR on private-practice disposals, annual allowance, pensionability of private income.

- For Locum Doctors: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/for-locum-doctors
  Self-assessment (SA103), IR35 for NHS Trust engagements, locum pension forms A and B, expenses, MTD from April 2026.

- For Junior Doctors: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/for-junior-doctors
  PAYE, self-assessment for locum/private sessions on top of NHS employment, expenses, early NHS pension decisions.

- Free Practice Health Check: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/free-practice-health-check
  No-cost diagnostic review of GP practice finances, tax position, and NHS pension exposure.

- Medical Guides: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/medical-guides
  In-depth reference guides on GP practice incorporation, locum tax, and NHS pension planning.

- Locations: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/locations
  Regional service pages for GPs and medical professionals across the UK.

## Free Calculators

- NHS Pension Annual Allowance Calculator: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/calculators/nhs-pension-annual-allowance
  Calculates your pension input amount against the £60,000 annual allowance and taper threshold; flags a potential scheme-pays charge.

- Locum Tax Calculator: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/calculators/locum-tax-calculator
  Income tax and Class 4 NIC estimate for sole-trader locum doctors (2026/27 rates).

- Private Practice Incorporation Calculator: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/calculators/private-practice-incorporation
  Compares sole-trader vs limited-company net take-home on private income, including the 2026/27 dividend rate rise and pension-accrual loss.

- All Calculators: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/calculators

## Highest-Value Blog Posts

- Becoming a GP Partner, Financial Implications: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/blog/becoming-gp-partner-financial-implications
  Capital buy-in, profit-share taxation (SA800/SA104), drawings vs profit, NHS pension as a partner, goodwill rules, and the last-man-standing premises risk.

- GP Partner vs Salaried GP, Tax Comparison: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/blog/gp-partner-vs-salaried-gp-tax-comparison
  Side-by-side income tax, NIC, pension, and take-home comparison for the two most common GP employment structures.

- GP Partnership Tax, Complete Guide: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/blog/gp-partnership-tax-complete-guide
  Full walkthrough of GP partnership accounts: profit allocation, basis-period reform, drawings policy, capital accounts, year-end tax reserving.

- NHS Pension Annual Allowance, Complete Guide: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/blog/nhs-pension-annual-allowance-complete-guide
  Annual allowance £60,000, taper (threshold income £200,000 / adjusted £260,000), pension input amount for DB schemes, scheme pays, carry-forward.

- Locum Doctor Tax, Complete Guide: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/blog/locum-doctor-tax-complete-guide
  Self-assessment, allowable expenses, locum pension forms A and B, IR35 for NHS Trust work, MTD for ITSA from April 2026.

- Buying Into a GP Partnership, Capital Parity Explained: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/blog/buying-into-gp-partnership-capital-parity-explained
  How capital parity works, financing a buy-in, tax relief on loan interest, and the risks of underfunded capital accounts.

- GP Limited Company, Tax Benefits and Drawbacks: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/blog/gp-limited-company-tax-benefits-drawbacks
  When incorporation of private work makes sense (and when it does not): corporation tax, 2026/27 dividend rates, pension-accrual loss, IR35 exposure.

- NHS Pension Scheme Pays, Doctors Deadlines: https://www.medicalaccounts.co.uk/blog/nhs-pension-scheme-pays-doctors-deadlines
  Mandatory vs voluntary scheme pays, the 31 July election deadline, benefit reduction mechanics, and the McCloud interaction.
